title: Tahara and Jewish Burial: Honoring the Dead
title_zh: 塔哈拉与犹太葬礼：敬重逝者
summary: An exploration of tahara (ritual purification) and Jewish burial practices, examining the sacred work of the Chevra Kadisha, the meaning behind burial customs, and how Jewish traditions honor the deceased with dignity and respect.
summary_zh: 探索塔哈拉（仪式净化）和犹太葬礼习俗，审视圣洁会的神圣工作、葬礼习俗背后的意义，以及犹太传统如何以尊严和尊重敬重逝者。
summary_he: חקירה של טהרה (טיהור טקסי) ונוהגי קבורה יהודיים, בחינת העבודה הקדושה של חברא קדישא, המשמעות מאחורי מנהגי קבורה, וכיצד מסורות יהודיות מכבדות את הנפטרים בכבוד ובהערכה.
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge
cultural_parallels: Confucian values of honoring the body and respecting ancestors
Chinese funeral washing and dressing customs
Community mutual aid societies (hui) similar to Chevra Kadisha
Comparison between natural decomposition vs. preservation practices

sources.title: Talmud, Moed Katan 27b
sources.description: On the simplicity of burial garments

sources.title: Genesis 1:27
sources.description: Humanity created in the image of God

sources.title: Genesis 3:19
sources.description: Dust to dust - return to earth

sources.title: Shulchan Aruch, Yoreh De'ah 352-362
sources.description: Laws regarding the treatment of the deceased

sources.title: Gesher HaChaim
sources.author: Rabbi Yechiel Michel Tukachinsky
sources.description: Comprehensive guide to Jewish mourning practices

sources.title: Jewish Insights into Death and Mourning
sources.author: Rabbi Maurice Lamm
sources.description: Contemporary guide for understanding Jewish practices around death
